reviews.review_title: Great game that will only get better.
reviews.review_description: Enjoyed many aspects of the game, the climbing was good and has been patched to be better. The melee combat was fun and feels powerful. The bow and arrow was accurate and enjoyable to shoot. Had my wow moments at times with the depths of view and quality of some of the more open areas. Really enjoyed using the stones to grapple around and parkour about. Liked the game and look forward to all the upcoming patches.

reviews.review_title: FROM ROUGH GEM TO VR EPIC!
reviews.review_description:
I first played Ember Souls during its beta phase and already saw huge potential. Now, after many updates and improvements, it's amazing to see how far the game has come.

This adventure successfully blends parkour, sword combat, magic powers, exploration, puzzles, and epic boss battles into a rich VR experience. The world is beautiful, immersive, and full of memorable moments that make you feel like the hero of your own fantasy adventure.

What impresses me most is the dedication of the developers. They have continuously improved the game with bug fixes, better combat, improved interactions, cloud saves, new voices, gameplay enhancements, and additional content. Their commitment to the community truly deserves recognition.

Ember Souls now feels polished, ambitious, and incredibly fun to play. If you enjoy action-adventure games inspired by classics like Prince of Persia or Assassin's Creed, this is an experience you should absolutely try.

Congratulations to the development team for their hard work and passion. Ember Souls has become one of the most impressive VR adventures available today.

I really want to love this game but there's just some things that make it feel so weird 😭

1. You can sometimes glitch out map
2. When you grab things your arms sometimes go long
3. And The enemies can sometimes feel weird fighting

But overall this game does have potential

reviews.review_title: PoP + Dungeons of Eternity, but lacking.
reviews.review_description:
This is a rough... but fun experience primarily because it provides traversal that leads to pretty engaging navigational puzzles similar to "prince of persia sands of time". Its not necessarily janky, but it clearly lacks polish. It's literally an upolished "Dungeons of Eternity". Same mechanics but with the addition of parkour. The campaign is pretty short... but fun. Combat is... rough polish wise, enemies run into eachothers weapons and you really can just use this by walking around with you sword pointed forward... swinging not necessary lol. The endgame procedural generated dungeons(raid) are very VERY underwhelming. If you noticed the lack of polish in the campaign... its even more apparent here. The dungeons only consist of two themes/biomes and are essentially just hand crafted rooms shuffled together randomly. Enemy placement is the same everytime... and somehow the maps feel "barren" somehow... Sure, there is infinite replayability, but its lazy and after like 2 runs, you most likely wont hop in afain as there is no reward or progression outside a leaderboard.

Buy it on sale for sure... not at full price though.
The parkour is fun and unique enough to get you through the campaign... but theres not much to this package after that.
Great enemies and a sense of immersion. Violence and gore makes ir fun with good combat

It's the closest  game to Prince of Persia you can get in VR and it's pretty good after all the updates. Make sure to change settings if you have problems with parkour.. Combat is simple but you are given a lot of  different  weapons and ways to fight. Some glitches here and there but nothing serious. There are four different game modes so you won't get bored quickly plus the campaign is around 8 hours itself. The game is definitely worth your money and time.

This is so much fun and for the sale price it's a no brainer grab it. You climb, grapple, slide down ropes, wall run, slide down drapes, shoot arrows smash pots and get in sword fights. Great ragdoll physics, plenty of settings to configure the gameplay and you can either have floating hands, arms and hands, or a full body. The voice acting is really good and you don't have to stop and listen to cutscenes everything's done right.

This game is excellent as far as I'm concerned and especially at the current price. The graphics are beautiful, the combat is satisfying and I really love the parkour. The story is great also and there is a rich variety of enemies and npcs. I haven't run into any serious bugs either. This game is easily worth much more than the current price point of 5.99.

Hope the devs see this. I really love what they did with this game. It’s pure fun. It has all the things that make vr fun. No lengthy cut scenes to stand around and watch either. I bought the game at lunch and it was kinda buggy. I replayed it this week and it’s a much better game. Sill with some bugs but nothing worth mentioning. Can’t wait to see what this studio cooks up next!!

developer_response.body:
Hi Juice5522! 

Thank you so much for the kind words! We are really happy to hear you are enjoying the game :) 

If you have any feedback, suggestions, or bug reports, we would love to hear from you. Please feel free to join our Discord community here: https://discord.gg/RQcrfcfjj9

Thanks again for supporting the game! 

Cheers

reviews.review_description:
PROS: Very good ambientation + Parkour is cool + Graphics are good for a standalone + You have bow, swords and shields to decide how to fight + Abilities and puzzles add variety + Eagle vision + Music + Long game for his price + The more you go forward the more the game is better

CONS: Issues grabbing things + You need to duck to take coins and they are a bit useless  + Chest are no rewarding + Combat is so so + Puzzles are easy + A bit repetitive

It’s a good game. It’s simple but beautiful and has puzzles that’s don’t make you wanta give up being too hard. It’s somewhat short but fun. It’s got great graphics for not being pc. I’m glad I got it. I assume I’ll replay it again later given i have short term memory and it will feel new again in year or so lol. The mechanics of slowing time on a jump that’s important is something I loved about it. I played it on easy and felt like an butt whooping deity. If like action rpgs then it’s a must try
I had fun.

reviews.review_title: Good story thus far, height set is off
reviews.review_description: Game is pretty good and good story. Height setting is off though, either too high or low into the floor, even with offset you can do. Had to reset game several times to get height to being close to right. Wish there was a crouch option. Combat is a bit janky as some have mentioned. Also having trouble on calling the falcon. I went into the second room that you need to use falcon vision but have no way of calling him. The directions say call the falcon, extend your arms and press y to enter vision. However, I’ve extended my arms, pressed every button on the controller, but see no way to call the falcon.  If this was introduced earlier in the game, I must have missed it. There should be a controller map on the settings menu to remind you how to call the falcon if it takes some special way to do it.
It wasn’t the most graphically intensive game ever, but felt decent and gives me a Prince of Persia feeling in VR.  Good effort.  Not that bad a game.  Ran good on Meta 3

Ember Souls offers a campaign, sandbox and raid mode. Raids are procedurally generated maps that you can speedrun. In sandbox mode you can test the combat. Campaign is a six hour long adventure with a bit of story - you are Kasim, the last immortal who is meant to gather all Ember stones, embrace their power and beat the Shadow Sultan. 

Primary gameplay is parkour. The player can climb, wall run, jump, swing between poles or slide down tapestries. You can view the palace rooms as puzzles the player needs to navigate using parkour-style movement. Unfortunately there is one set path so there is not much freedom of movement. It starts with tutorial-like areas but the deeper we get, the harder is to navigate the rooms.

Ember stones we collect give us two abilities that can be used both in combat and our platforming adventure. The first abilities we get is air push that can destroy items or hit enemies. The second ability doubles as a grappling hook. Using the Water stone we can freeze water, Fire stone can help us burn stuff and Earth stone will gives us the power to produce our own vines that we can scale.  

The palace is guarded and we need to beat the enemies in order to advance. Combat system is a simple hack'n'slash. While Ember stone abilities can make it more interesting, there is mostly no need to use special abilities or different weapons which leaves us with boring repetitive combat. Boss battles require you to do something specific but the combat is not fully functional so it can become very frustrating or boring to beat the boss.

Enemies leave behind coins which can be used to buy new weapons/armor. The weapons don't do anything interesting and feel not worth the money. 

Ember Souls's atmosphere and visuals are beautiful. Soundtrack and sound design are very nice too. The voice acting though is not good and can break the immersion.

Don't forget to pet the bird, he purrs.

Honestly, this is my biggest V.R surprise in the last year. I was interested in this game when it was announced, but Youtube reviews kind of put me off from buying. I got this game because it was on sale and as I'd just returned another turgid new release that I'd been waiting on since time immemorial, I had Quest-Cash to spend. First off it was incredibly nice to be greeted with so many settings and QOL options. Especially after the game I returned had hardly any and committed the cardinal sin of stating that it had a seated mode, when, as I am increasingly seeing it actually didn't: It had midget mode instead. AKA no height offset, no actual seated mode and the fashionable notion that it is acceptable to play as Tiny Tom Cruise  in a land of giants, giant doors and switches that I can't reach. Ember Souls, is not like that. It's slick, smooth, has some remarkable ideas about the implementation of parkour,  has some very endearing NPC pals, an authentically evocative Arabian Knights/prince of Persia vibe and lovely graphics. The music here is also immense, along with the voice-acting and sound design in general. The game is also, most importantly outrageously fun and fluid. The object physics are satisfying and convincing, Immersion is high and there are some beautiful swashbuckling mechanics and touches: Sliding down a wall tapestry to slow you enough to not die when you hit bottom, whilst your nails rake the fabric as you fall. The climbing is actually fun. I also noticed that pretty much every change or option or QOL feature reviewers on this page have asked for in the past, have been provided. This is quality work and the Devs are as talented as they are  receptive. I can't wait to see what they do next.
3 stars for now.

developer_response.body:
Hi DevilBrandon, 

We have released a new update fixing the skill points cap, you should be able to  unlock everything now :)
 
Cheers!

reviews.review_description:
You may think it's a prince of Persia game but it is more of a bad version of Behemoth and Assassin's Creed with some Spiderman notes.
The story seems interesting but quite ordinary and not that deep. The graphics are basic and the areas are all the same. 
The parkour part is not accurate and has many glitches. The fighting system is chunky but there are cool animations and physics while stabbing and pushing. The NPCs, the bosses are a joke, even in hard mode. The puzzles are too simple.
The magic part is good but not much diversity for the game progression (you use primarily the air swinging technique). The weapons are not that interesting (no damage difference and only one has a unique special ability other than stronger hits).
Having sufficient coins is difficult and you finish the game without all the abilities upgrades. I guess you can use the sandbox and the raids modes but, surprise, it gets too laggy and unplayable after a while.
All in all, I enjoyed playing the game but I wouldn't really recommend it. Not at full price at least.

I don't think I have a more gorgeous game in my library.  I'm using a Quest 2 so the graphics are not the "best" due to hardware restrictions and it's still spectacular!  I get motion sick with walking games and this doesn't affect me.  Love, love, LOVE it!  I have to stop playing for now since my old Q2 controller has a drifting stick so when I swing the sword, I end up snapping my back to the enemy.  I will definitely pick this up on a different device. :)

What I like about the game is that the physics of the tax are very great grabbing on makes it very simple. When you moving the character finding the treasures and the keys is a good challenge. Also, the puzzles are great. I really don’t see nothing wrong with the game it can always do updates But overall, it’s a great game to play. I recommended 🔥￼￼

I tried my best to love this game. I forced myself to keep playing, hoping to find something engaging — but it was all in vain.

The paradox of this game is that you can see its huge potential, but within the first 15 minutes, it already feels boring. It has a beautiful atmosphere, but no real immersion. There’s a variety of mechanics, but there are too many of them, and most don’t work properly. The combat is decent in theory, but incredibly dull. There’s parkour, but it gets old after five minutes. There are puzzles, but they just make you yawn.

It’s like the game has everything it needs to be good, but everything feels half-baked.

Overall: a major disappointment. Prince of Persia on the Apple II and NES is far more enjoyable (and I’m not exaggerating).

Like Prince of Persia? Cool, go and play that instead. Because whilst this game can simply and succinctly be summed up as Prince of Persia in VR, it's broken to the point of being unplayable. It gets stuck on loading screens often, features countless glitches, and my progress has been halted by having to climb a ladder that teleports me out of the map and then resets me at the bottom of the ladder. Shame, because there seems to be a fun game in here, but don't support developers who put out such a lacklustre product.

developer_response.body:
Hi PerfectRice, we're very sorry that you didn't have a good first experience. 

Could you please give us more information about the glitches and specifically the ladder that teleports you please? We are constantly improving the game and your feedback is very important to us.  

Please feel free to join our Discord server at https://discord.gg/RQcrfcfjj9 to share your thoughts directly with us and our community. Alternatively, you can reach out via email at support@virtualage.com

Thanks

reviews.review_title: Eh... it was $ 9.99. So there's that.
reviews.review_description: Its fine. I would be disappointed if it i paid $20, but I got it on sale for $9.99.

I bought Ember Souls a few weeks ago but just got around to playing it the other day. I am having a lot of fun with my time in Ember Souls. It is like Prince of Persia and Asgard’s Wrath 2 combined into one game. where this game shines the most,at least to me,is the parkour aspect. Though the parkour is my favorite,the combat system is quite solid as well. I loved the weapons as well as the powers you get. Don’t even get me started on your talking bird companion who purs like a cat when you pet it. This game definitely deserves more love from the community. If you’re reading this,go ahead and buy it,,I highly recommend this game

I really like the concept of this game but it just feels too indie to be enjoyable.

The combat, parkour, inventory, and basic VR interactions feel primitive and clumsy. For example even something as common in VR as picking up objects with force grab is a game of hit or miss. 

The flatly lit environments and repetitive enemy designs make it difficult to become immersed in the game.

I appreciate all the thought that went into it but the end result is underwhelming and it’s frustrating to play.

Even at the sale price of 50% off I’m requesting a refund because I have no desire to play it any further.

Fun game but needs work. 
Mechanics are clunky at times which ruins the flow of gameplay. 
Very frustrating. 
Not a value for 25 bucks. 
Maybe 15 dollars.
Wanted so much more then I got. It is a good game just feels lifeless, would love an update! they definitely put effort into this just not as much as I would like.

Game is great and great graphics and design detail.

Puzzles are good and combat feels good too, no issues.

Leveling up is great and upgrading weapons by collecting coins is great.

Seems like a lengthy game, which is awesome.

Nice little touches, seeing through the birds eyes and various hand magic spells.

Not much negativity to really say, seems polished and a great adventure so far.

I would recommend this.

Thought I'd try it after all this time. Wanted something knew and it looked like it could have been fun. 
The graphics are pretty decent and crisp.
Its combat and enemies feels rather uninspired and stiff.
The parkour part seems to work as intended. I have only been able to play the intro run, and then the game froze. But I was already kinda bored, going through the motions. So I'm gonna refund, since it won't be able to hold my attention long enough.

Could be a fun game for some, not for me. So 3*

Really enjoying this game. The story is engaging, mechanics all work well, weapons and upgrades are also plentiful. The combat feels good and can get very gruesome at some points. The wall jumping and swinging feel really good once you get the hang of it. I'm pleased with the game and feel this was a solid purchase.
I hope this is gonna be great. It looks amazing and I’m just excited to play Because I haven’t played it yet￼
Had some hiccups when I first tried it. I came back after some months and now it plays well. Sometimes crashes on bigger fights with more enemies, that would be the only thing that needs to be fixed.

This is a true action adventure for all VR lovers. The mechanics are easy to understand so that all level of players can find something they like. A Campaign mode, Sandbox mode, and I believe the upcoming mode to be a Mixed Reality mode! I love the Parkour mechanics and is great fun once you master it. Or at least, once I mastered it!! LOL
It's true there is a bit of jank sometimes like, items going through floors and such but not so much that it breaks the game. I also believe these issues will be taken care of in the future, as I believe they are working hard to make this game, the best it can be.
The Atmosphere is wonderful, and the music gives you the feel like you Are" the prince of Persia" but with Really cool abilities!
Don't hesitate to buy this game and get hours of fun ahead!
Whoop, Whoop!

I really enjoy this game. If it was just the gameplay, it would be an easy 5 for me. In fact, I think it will be a 5 soon. The first patch fixed a lot, but there are still enough small issues that I can't quite give it a 5. There are fun puzzles (I love that), but it might be a touch too helpful with the solutions. On the other hand I've seen several side paths to secret areas that I haven't figured out. There are frantic battles. I can regularly cheese win, but I'm playing on the easiest level for a reason. Don't judge. I'm enjoying the combat and I could make it harder if I wanted to. I like the graphics, even on the Quest 2. (I have a Q3; I'm not using it for this for unimportant reasons.) It's not the perfect game, but I can't fault the fun.

Update: I've completed the campaign and my opinion is about the same. The fun is a 5, but even though the devs have worked hard to fix issues, it still has too many minor, non game breaking flaws to be a 5 star game. I still recommend playing it.

developer_response.body:
Hi tpancoast,

Thank you so much for your review and feedback!

We’d love to hear more details about the issues that prevented you from fully enjoying the game and giving it 5 stars. Your input is very important as we work to make the game the best it can be :)

Please feel free to join our Discord server at https://discord.gg/RQcrfcfjj9 to share your thoughts directly with us and our community. Alternatively, you can reach out via email at support@virtualage.com.

Thanks again for your support, and we look forward to hearing more from you!

Cheers

reviews.review_description: Can you add an option In the menu where you can disable the use of abilities in any hand your holding something in? It would make throwing easier especially when the wind stone is equipped

I’ve been playing the game for about 5 hours now, and I can confidently say that once you master the parkour mechanics,the game becomes truly amazing. The combat is a blast, offering engaging melee options with swords and a hammer, as well as ranged combat using throwing knives and arrows. On top of that, using magic adds an extra layer of fun to the gameplay!!!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review!

reviews.review_description:
A VR cross between Prince of Persia and Assassins Creed. Very fun with lots of replayability and different modes.

A very enjoyable and fun campaign where you unlock powers as you traverse different palace levels defeating the odd boss. A decent enough story with some good (if not great) fighting against various enemies. The campaign alone is well worth a second playthrough with all the powers unlocked. 

But what sets it apart is the excellent parkour (this takes practice btw) and the extra modes. Sandbox allows you to practice against waves of enemies and Raid is randomly generated mini maps which you can either speed run or search for chests and gold to unlock new weapons and items.

At time of writing the developers are still actively working on updates to improve any glitches and bugs (having already released a few). It really deserves to be rated a bit higher but it was maybe released a little bit early so suffered a few lower scores for issues that have now already been fixed.

In traditional 2d games I'm not a big parkour fan, but in this VR game I find it amazing.  Swinging from flag pole to flag pole, landing on a wall banner to slide down.... I wasn't just controlling the games protagonist, I WAS the hero.  It truly felt like I was in the game world and doing all these things, rather than controlling an in-game character to do the things.  The difference was exhilarating.  Had me grinning like a little kid (and its been several decades since I was either little or a kid).  The combat is swords, bows, magic.  You unlock more skills as you progress which I enjoy.  Campaign mode was my favorite.  Love the character progression and storytelling.  There are two other modes- a sandbox for spawning in enemies to practice your moves on and a roguelike wave battler to try and get higher and higher score.  I was in between a 4 or 5 rating since I did come across some minor bugs.  I decided to go with the 5 rating because the bugs were minor and went away quickly.  If you're looking for adventure and fun I highly recommend this game for a healthy dose of both.

reviews.review_title: Amazing game, but can be buggy.
reviews.review_description:
I want to preface this review by stating that I have completed the story mode and had immense fun while doing so.

Ember Souls is a fun, hack and slash vr game, reminiscent of the 3d Prince of Persia titles. A lot of care was put into making this a great game, and it shows, as I had fun from the beginning to the end. The swordplay was fun, the puzzles were enjoyable (although on the simpler side, but not a negative), and the parkour was absolutely thrilling (when it worked). The parkour was fun, but sometimes frustrating, in that about half the time I used the wind stone to grapple to the next pole or beam, I could not grab them, or would even pass right through them, falling to my death. Sometimes, the momentum of falling would be carried after I died, and I would be flung up into the air, only to die again. A big problem that I had was that when I entered the Baths 3, the textures and geometry completely disappeared, even after restarting the game multiple times. I had to load from a checkpoint, and only then did it load properly.

Other than that, I have no complaints. The enemies were varied and challenging, and fun to find each one of their weaknesses. The weapons are beautifully modeled, as well as the environments. My GOD, the environments! They are absolutely spectacular, and immersed me deep into the world. The traps were fun to figure out and then see how fast I could get through them. The story was quite enjoyable, and left me with a cast of characters I won’t forget. 

Overall, this game is an 8/10 for me, and is a must play, regardless of any of the bugs or glitches that may be present.

Its basic, short and missing character variety. The voice acting is genuinely bad… like distracting bad. Maybe even unintentionally comical.

I get that B&S might be the standard that everyone should live by, but this was a cheap attempt. 

The game is incredibly easy once you tweak the controller settings. Also, you can only climb on designated areas, its not really open for free exploration, even if the developers say otherwise. 

Physics need a lot of improvement.  

If you have played everything else thats similar, then this is worth a try… i wish I skipped this one.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for your feedback, it’s important to us.

The voices were re-recorded in the latest patch with new actors. If there are any you still think could be improved, we’d be very interested to know which ones you find lacking.

Additionally, in Settings / Game / Difficulty, you can increase the difficulty and disable parkour assists if you feel they make things too easy.

The part of the game with the most open exploration is Raids. We encourage you to try it and share your feedback with us.

New enemies appear as you progress through the campaign, and in next week’s update, we’ll be adding more enemies along with additional combat animations for the existing ones

Please feel free to join our Discord server at https://discord.gg/RQcrfcfjj9 to share your thoughts directly with us and our community. Alternatively, you can reach out via email at support@virtualage.com.

reviews.review_description:
Ember Souls takes you to the Orient and a palace brimming with secrets. What schemes does the Sultan hide behind his walls? Does it fulfill the dreams of a Prince of Persia experience in VR? Let me tell you this: you'll love the variety in gameplay and see firsthand why VR is a true game changer. 

This game delivers countless goosebump moments that completely draw you in. Rarely has it been this hard for me to take off my VR headset.

Technically, I’m treated to stunning dynamic lighting, and yes, I actually clapped during the intro when I saw the ride to the palace. The graphics are beautifully crafted, with so much love for detail.  

The soundtrack is pure love.

but it makes you dizzy quite a bit
This is first application where I need to stop playing after only 10 minutes… 8-(

reviews.review_title: An interesting take on a physical game
reviews.review_description:
Now, this game was quite interesting when I first acquired it and tbh, I didn't have many expectations for it since it looked quite stereotypical in what it had to offer.

HOWEVER

What I was more suprised to learn is that it's actually NOT THAT BAD. It's mechanics and combat is overall quite robust and the switch and mix between them both is overall quite decent. There is also some pretty good filler too like a sandbox since the game is physics - based and I will say, it's NOT half bad!

There are the odd hiccups every now and again where maybe your not knowing what your doing or what the story is but I feel the games parkour and combat aspect makes up for that a bit.

Three stars ^^

🎖🎖🎖

This game is literally just Blade and Sorcery: Nomad but it’s actually bad. first of all, it’s ‘parkour’ is literally just pre-programmed movement along walls and ripping/climbing curtains, so they have ZERO room to let people be creative, unlike Blade and Sorcery. Also, why is there no free climb? weapons also have bad physics, especially in your own hands.

developer_response.body:
Hi CawesomePro2011, 
 
The parkour is not pre-programmed :) You can grab on a column and propel your self in any direction for example, or jump mid air from one beam to another like spiderman with the grappling hook (please try the Raids mode for a more parkour focused game mode), only wall runs and courtains have predefined trajectories. 

If you have any specific suggestions on how to improve the game we would love to hear your feedback. You can write us at support@virtualage.com or join our discord server here: https://discord.gg/RQcrfcfjj9

Cheers

reviews.review_title: More of an adventure than a souls-like.
reviews.review_description: I played on the easy setting, but most gamers will probably want to use the 2 higher difficulty levels.  The music is great, the visuals are great, the settings have lots of options to play the way you want.  The game takes checkpoint saves quite frequently so you don't lose much progress when you die.  The game teaches you how to play as you go along.  If you want tons of action you may be a bit disappointed, but there is a nice combination of exploration, collecting, platforming, fighting, and a tiny bit of puzzling.

reviews.review_title: The Bird Purrs Like A Cat!
reviews.review_description: Ember Souls is a whole lot of game for the money.  The campaign is fairly lengthy, and there are other game modes that add replay value that I haven't even dabbled with yet (I'm still working through the campaign).  First off, no this isn't AC Nexus.  The parkour doesn't feel as fluid, and the combat doesn't feel as good either, but it's also far cheaper to buy than AC Nexus.  There's a lot to like about Ember Souls.  The combat is fun (I prefer the bow and arrow myself), and the abilities you get can be upgraded as you play opening up new ways to approach a fight.  The puzzles aren't frustrating to solve and the levels feel well designed while looking very nice.  This game has everything:  puzzles, parkour, combat, boss fights, collectables, etc.  This is definitely a "just one more level" game, where it's hard to put down once you start playing!

I was really looking forward to this game, like Prince of Persia in VR. It's got climbing, scaling, traps, coins, weapons and baddies. It's got magic and talking birds. I really felt pulled into the game, or at least I almost was. As I played more multiple bugs kept getting in the way of enjoying the game. Once they polish off those bugs this is a 5 star, I look forward to that.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review Slim1337!

If you’d like to share any issues you found or have any suggestions for improving the game, we would love to hear your feedback! You can write us at support@virtualage.com or join our discord server here: https://discord.gg/RQcrfcfjj9

We are constantly improving it and your feedback is very valuable to us :)

Thank you for supporting us!

reviews.review_title: Amazing game!!! One of the best Vr
reviews.review_description: Loved the game. 5 hours in almost done 80% and don’t want it to be over it’s so good! Please make a sequel!! Definitely don’t listen tot he bad reviews. Probably before the devs patched it.
Don't bother. I had constant glitching trying to climb things. Not good enough for retail release.

developer_response.body:
We're very sorry that your experience wasn't great. We're a bit surprised by the issues you've encountered, as parkour is very well received in the game.

Here are some tips that might improve your experience:

- Try calibrating your height (in Settings / Body / Calibrate Height).
- Make sure the jump helpers are enabled in Settings / Difficulty, as they can be very helpful.
- In settings / Game / disable holsters when climbing

If you still feel there are areas that could be improved, we'd love to know which specific points you think need adjustment.

Please feel free to join our Discord server at https://discord.gg/RQcrfcfjj9 to share your thoughts directly with us and our community. Alternatively, you can reach out via email at support@virtualage.com

reviews.review_description: I really liked playing through the game. I found a ton of glitches. Like a ton. but it was a ton of fun lol. I really liked the weapons for the game. but I wish it had a bit more control for the ranged weapons. I really hope later on in the games life they can get real voice actors. I really like the games mechanics and the wind one the most I feel like the other stones things don't have enough. Or hmm they Don't feel as cool. Or exciting to get and use. There was this one glitch were if you got to the settings change your hight offset to as low as you can and crouch so your jest below the floor level and than put your hands in the middle of the floor amd external abyss you go flying across the room and lunched out of the world before it spawnd you back in the map lol. I really liked the game and can't wait for more to come to the game and future updates. And to find a whole ton more bugs to smash.

Ember Souls feels like it was born out of a need for a new Prince Of Persia game that we never got. It's a really enjoyable several hour game that puts you right into the jumping, swinging, wall running and combat of a 2003 classic. 

The main character Kasim travels to a castle that has been overrun by an evil emperor. He’s greeted by a magical falcon who guides him as he scales castle towers, fighting his way through many combat encounters and platforming and puzzle sections. There is plenty of variety that cycles in and out, but it does feel like it borrows a bit too heavily on the Prince Of Persia gameplay elements and mechanics. 

The movement felt really solid during platforming, and just generally running around. I never felt like I didn’t have full control of my characters movement, and was nearly always able to have consistent situational awareness. Being able to jump in and tweak the sliders a bit let me zero in on the proper feel of my movement, and customize it to my liking. 

Being able to easily target weapons and recall them from a distance also was a nice touch as it felt as if there was nothing out of my reach. I constantly have issues in other games where I can’t grab something simple, especially in a seated position in front of a desk, but that was never an issue in this game. It also made combat encounters much more enjoyable as well than the standard fair in the melee action adventure genre. 

The combat was something I initially didn’t find too engaging. It felt fine, but I also have an issue with VR games that have a very floaty style to the melee. Arms usually feel very rubbery and seem like it never maps 1:1. While the sword and shield combat isn’t anything special. It’s when I started thinking outside the box a bit, that I started to really enjoy it. The ability to throw knives and swords really amps up the speed and enjoyment of the frantic multiple enemy encounters. And being able to spin around, toss weapons into the air and catch them w

reviews.review_title: A charming title with a lot of potential
reviews.review_description:
I will be clear upfront and say that this game (currently) has many quirks and flaws which can make it frustrating to play at times. If you are prone to getting upset over QoL issues, you should likely wait before purchasing this.

Keep an eye on it, however, as the developers are actively addressing issues and frequently updating the game to improve the player experience.
The characters currently use AI-generated voice lines, which may be a deal-breaker for many folk, but the developers have stated that they will be trying to replace them soon.
If you are not bothered by AI voice lines, or minor QoL issues, you should try this game. It has very responsive swordplay, interesting mechanics, and a very cute bird.

If you are looking for a classic action/adventure platforming game in VR, this will be right up your alley. The platforming, swinging, and climbing all mix together with some other interesting mechanics to make engaging puzzles which may take you a moment to figure out. But if you get stuck, the developers have included a hint system. The difficulty of the game is highly customizable, and even on the highest difficulty the combat has a good pace (for the most part. I am looking at you, first boss).

Overall, even if you do not pick up the game immediately - I highly recommend keeping an eye on it, as it has the potential to be fantastic once it has be well polished.

developer_response.body:
Hi thatonepeasantgirl!

Thank you so much for your review and feedback :) 

If you wouldn’t mind, we’d love to hear more details about the QoL issues you mentioned. Your input would be very helpful as we finalize the next update and improve the game. 

Feel free to join our Discord server at https://discord.gg/RQcrfcfjj9, where you can share feedback directly with us and our community, or you can also send us an email at support@virtualage.com

Thanks again for your support, and we look forward to hearing more from you.

Cheers!

reviews.review_title: Best POP game Ubisoft never made
reviews.review_description:
This is hands down my favorite game on my 3S. The implementation of the platforming is utterly beautiful; you really actually do feel like you're climbing, swinging, jumping, and traversing through an enormous Persian palace. All the classic platforming components are here (and done well), wall runs, rope drops, pole swings, banner slides, long complex sequences of precise actions you have to complete to traverse the areas. The magic is done well too, the classic time freeze power is here from SOT, along with the "turn water to ice so you can traverse it" mechanic from Forgotten Sands.

The combat is (to me) perfect; depending on how you're feeling that day (or how exhausted your arms already are) you can change options so  combat is anything from a pretty realistic and exhausting experience to a hilarious jank fest.

Honestly super impressed with this title, and grateful someone else decided to create this game so I can play it!!!! I highly, highly recommend.

It’s a great game and the spells and puzzles are great! But it has a couple things that I think need some fixing like most enemies can’t block a jab and you take a lot of fall damage for falling off really low and sometimes the enemies go through the floor if you hit them right and over all it’s a great game and I would tell my friend to play it

The concept is fantastic however the execution well its a little lacking theres issues with piercing enemies collisions when climbing that really destroy immersion the combat hitboxes are a little janky and need working on so does the throwing mechanics as when i throw my dagger and it hits them it dosent always go through which is weird also the gameplay can stutter from time to time even on my quest 3s but the game is promising and is fun even with these issues the best part is the ember stones the force push move on the wind stone was a whole lot of fun i believe this game has potential it just needs a bit of rework in the mechanics! 5.6 out of 10 for me
This game is a huge improving to the gladiator game gladius

Ember Souls is an amazing game that reminds me a lot of Blade & Sorcery; however, this game is so much better in many ways. First of all, it has a fully voiced story spread across 18 levels. The entire game takes 6 to 8 hours to beat and has great replayability with raid and sandbox modes. The combat is fun, smooth, and very well done. For example, you can block enemy sword slashes by holding your sword perpendicular to theirs, dismember enemy limbs, throw daggers, shoot arrows, and use 8 elemental spells, like wind, fire, water, and earth. The boss fights were stunning with each boss having a specific mechanic to kill them, similar to zelda games. 

The graphics were pretty good for a standalone Quest game and the game was well optimized to have almost no fps issues on my Quest 3. The puzzles were superb, using VR very well, allowing you to push, turn, and grab objects to solve them. The parkour was also great with wall running, VR climbing and jumping, dodging traps, and sliding down ropes.

Did I mention that you can pet your bird familar and it purrs! This is one VR hack-and-slash game that you do not want to miss!

Honestly quite disappointed, the mechanics and graphics are kind of janky that’s not to say there is no polishing but the contrast is jarring , physics not working correctly vital mechanics like climbing and wall running barely functioning or not at all, bodies floating in the air etc etc , it’s a shame because the intent is there, it has potential but feels like an unfinished early access tech demo in all honesty, seeing as this is full release and you can only expect so much from updates I wouldn’t remain too hopeful going to refund when I can be bothered was optimistic but thoroughly disappointed , first game I played on my quest 3 , not sure if it’s somehow better on a 2 but doubtful save your time .

developer_response.body:
Hi Nels311,

We are sorry you didn't have a good first experience with the game.

If you have any suggestions on how to improve any parts of it we would love to hear your feedback! You can write us at support@virtualage.com or join our discord server here:
https://discord.gg/RQcrfcfjj9

We are constantly improving it and your feedback is very valuable to us :)

Thank you!

reviews.review_description: Ember Souls is an amazing "Prince of Persia" VR experience. From the parkour, the magic, the environments and the combat, everything comes together to deliver an exciting game. It's an incredible adventure experience.

reviews.review_title: very good but could be better
reviews.review_description:
the combats cool but it seems kinda basic like there’s not too much you can do with it beside hack and slash it could be really good with better physics and stabbing animations but i would give the combat a 7/10 
there was one bug i found when trying to load into missions where the game would freeze on the loading screen and i would have to restart the game but other than that it was fine
